        namespace analyze_temporal_data {

        struct Event {
          sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> event_id;
          std::string event_name;
          sqlgen::Timestamp<"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"> start_time;
          std::optional<sqlgen::Timestamp<"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S">> end_time;
          sqlgen::Timestamp<"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"> created_at;
        };

        struct TemporalAnalysis {
          int event_id;
          std::string event_name;
          int start_day;
          int start_month;
          int start_year;
          int start_hour;
          int start_minute;
          int start_second;
          int start_weekday;
          int64_t start_epoch;
          std::optional<double> duration_days;
        };

        std::vector<TemporalAnalysis> analyze_temporal_data (const std::vector<Event>& events)  {
          using namespace sqlgen::literals;

          // Connect to the database
          auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");

          // Create a temporary table for Event
          auto create_table_stmt = sqlgen::transpilation::to_create_table<Event>();
          dynamic::Statement stmt = create_table_stmt;
          std::string create_table_sql = conn->to_sql(stmt);
          create_table_sql = std::regex_replace(create_table_sql, std::regex("CREATE TABLE"), "CREATE TEMPORARY TABLE");

          // Insert the events
          conn->write(events);

              "event_id"_c,
              "event_name"_c,
              sqlgen::day("start_time"_c).as<"start_day">(),
              sqlgen::month("start_time"_c).as<"start_month">(),
              sqlgen::year("start_time"_c).as<"start_year">(),
              sqlgen::hour("start_time"_c).as<"start_hour">(),

              sqlgen::weekday("start_time"_c).as<"start_weekday">(),
              sqlgen::unixepoch("start_time"_c).as<"start_epoch">(),
              sqlgen::days_between("start_time"_c, "end_time"_c).as<"duration_days">()
          ) | sqlgen::where("end_time"_c.is_not_null());

          // Execute the query and get the results
          auto results = conn->read<std::vector<TemporalAnalysis>>(query).value();

          return results;
        }

        }  // namespace analyze_temporal_data

5. Execute the query and return the results.

- `user_id` (same as in UserProfile)

- `email_length` (the length of the trimmed email)

```cpp
#pragma once

#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>

namespace trim_and_length_validation {

struct UserProfile {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> user_id;
  std::string name;
  std::string email;
};

struct ValidatedProfile {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> user_id;
  std::string trimmed_name;
  std::string trimmed_email;
  int name_length;
  int email_length;
};

std::vector<ValidatedProfile> trim_and_length_validation() {
  using namespace sqlgen::literals;

  // Connect to the database
  auto conn_result = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");
  if (!conn_result.is_ok()) {
    return {}; // Return empty vector on error
  }
  auto conn = conn_result.value();

  // Build the query
  auto query = sqlgen::select_from<UserProfile>(
      "user_id"_c,
      sqlgen::trim("name"_c).as<"trimmed_name">(),
      sqlgen::trim("email"_c).as<"trimmed_email">(),
      sqlgen::length(sqlgen::trim("name"_c)).as<"name_length">(),
      sqlgen::length(sqlgen::trim("email"_c)).as<"email_length">()
  ) | sqlgen::to<std::vector<ValidatedProfile>>;

  // Execute the query
  auto result = query(conn);
  if (result.is_ok()) {
    return result.value();
  } else {
    return {};
  }
}

}  // namespace trim_and_length_validation
```
